description:
## Goal

Split the workspace-transition coverage out of `src/App.test.tsx` into focused test modules without changing behavior.

## Background

`App.test.tsx` now contains general shell/sidebar behavior plus a large atomic-workspace-transition matrix (pending, cancellation, retry banner, removal, stale transitions, startup ordering, and recovery). The file is difficult to navigate and makes it hard to see which tests protect a given lifecycle invariant.

## Intended shape

Keep shared render helpers/fixtures in a small test helper only if that reduces duplication. Split by user-visible behavior, not implementation internals—for example switching/pending, cancellation and recovery, and ordinary App navigation. Avoid a one-test-per-file split.

## Acceptance criteria

- Each workspace lifecycle behavior has a focused, discoverable test home.
- Tests remain renderer/integration tests; do not weaken assertions to unit-only checks.
- Test names retain the domain invariant they protect.
- `pnpm test`, `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit`, and scoped Ultracite pass.

## Non-goals

No product behavior, RPC contract, or transition-model refactor. `bsm-kia.6` owns any reducer/model extraction.

comments.text:
# bsm-kia.8 — Split App workspace transition tests by behavior

## Objective

Refactor the renderer/integration coverage currently concentrated in `src/App.test.tsx` into a small set of behavior-oriented App test modules. This is a test-organization change only: preserve the React rendering surface, mocked Tauri/RPC boundaries, async ordering controls, domain-invariant test names, assertions, and production behavior. Do not extract or change the renderer transition logic; that is deliberately deferred to `bsm-kia.6`.

## Evidence and constraints

- `src/App.test.tsx` currently contains 28 tests: nine ordinary Issue Explorer/sidebar-navigation tests and 19 workspace-related renderer integration tests.
- The workspace tests exercise the public renderer composition point: Tauri `workspace-transition` events, typed TauRPC methods/responses, the native picker boundary, `WorkspaceSelector`, and rendered Issue Explorer state. The split must retain this integration level rather than replacing tests with private `applyTransition` checks.
- `App.tsx` intentionally owns the current generation/ref guard protocol. In particular, it protects against stale generations, delayed same-generation Pending after success/failure, initial-load races, cancellation-versus-commit races, Current removal/reset, and snapshot/state pairing. These tests are the safety net that `bsm-kia.6` will use when extracting a pure model.
- `WorkspaceSelector.test.tsx` already owns selector component behavior. App-level tests should retain only wiring and cross-component outcomes (picker cancellation/default path, Current removal clearing the explorer, RPC/state response handling), not duplicate selector-only rendering coverage.
- Vitest uses jsdom and global cleanup from `src/test/setup.ts`; any common support must work with Vitest’s hoisted module mocks. Do not hide mocked-module registration in a way that lets `App` import before mocks are active.

## Target test layout

1. **`src/App.navigation.test.tsx` — ordinary App navigation**
   Move the nine sidebar/Issue Explorer behavior tests:
   - disabled controls and hidden counts during loading and load failure;
   - loaded counts/default All; command-backed zero Blocked and zero Ready;
   - active-view click behavior; Ready rendering; search preserving source counts; and no reload while selecting a preloaded view.

   This file has no workspace-transition ordering cases. It remains the discoverable home for App shell/sidebar behavior.

2. **`src/App.workspace-selection.test.tsx` — selection and catalog integration**
   Move the App-level selection behavior tests:
   - backend-provided MRU catalog ordering;
   - native picker cancellation and defaulting to the most-recent available path;
   - removing Current clearing the Issue Explorer to the empty chooser;
   - rejected switch refresh surfacing typed inline validation feedback.

   Preserve the existing assertions that prove App wires its picker/RPC callbacks and applies the resulting workspace state, while leaving component-only selector cases in `WorkspaceSelector.test.tsx`.

3. **`src/App.workspace-switching.test.tsx` — pending, successful commit, and ordering**
   Move the switch path and snapshot-ordering cases:
   - older-generation event rejection;
   - rejected switch retaining the prior snapshot;
   - Pending-before-success retaining A while showing B pending;
   - success-before-delayed-Pending preventing a mixed B identity/B snapshot state;
   - initial Issue Explorer load losing to a later committed switch.

   Keep explicit deferred RPC resolution and event-listener capture where the sequence itself is the invariant. Test names must continue to state the observable protection (prior snapshot, stale transition, or no mixed identity/data), not merely the delivery mechanism.

4. **`src/App.workspace-recovery.test.tsx` — cancellation and recoverable failures**
   Move the cancellation and recovery matrix:
   - Cancel control invocation while Pending;
   - retry banner rendering/dismissal and retry target replay;
   - retryable and validation failure terminal behavior when delayed Pending arrives;
   - cancel racing a committed switch before success publication, including the response that carries the matching snapshot;
   - workspace-memory retry publishing restored Issue Explorer data, both first-load and stale-rendered-list scenarios.

   These stay together because they all prove that failure/cancellation preserves or atomically replaces the visible workspace-and-snapshot unit and exposes recovery without browser reload.

## Shared test support

5. Add a narrowly scoped test-support module under `src/test/` (for example, `src/test/app-workspace-fixtures.ts`) for pure, reusable fixture builders and types:
   - `buildIssue`;
   - successful and failed `IssueExplorerLoadState` builders;
   - `WorkspaceState` builder; and
   - shared test-only transition payload/listener types if they remove repeated unsafe `unknown` declarations.

   Keep builders configurable through typed overrides and preserve today’s default data, so moving tests does not silently alter fixture semantics. Keep App module mocks and mock registration at each consuming test file’s top level (or otherwise verify their hoisting/import order) and dynamically import `App` only after the mocks are established. This avoids a shared helper accidentally loading the real RPC/dialog/event modules before `vi.mock` takes effect.

6. In each App behavior file, use a compact local reset/setup routine for the relevant mocks. Share only genuinely stable fixture construction; do not create a generic test framework that obscures each behavior’s RPC/event sequence. Preserve the existing `act`, `waitFor`, `userEvent`, accessibility queries, and all asynchronous assertions.

7. Delete `src/App.test.tsx` after every existing test has one—and only one—focused destination. Do not leave a compatibility wrapper or duplicate coverage. Confirm test discovery includes the renamed/new `*.test.tsx` files through the existing Vitest configuration.

## Verification

8. First run each new module while moving it, then run the complete required gates:

```bash
pnpm test
pnpm exec tsc --noEmit
pnpm exec ultracite check src/App.navigation.test.tsx src/App.workspace-selection.test.tsx src/App.workspace-switching.test.tsx src/App.workspace-recovery.test.tsx src/test/app-workspace-fixtures.ts
```

If the repository convention requires the script wrapper instead, use `pnpm run check` and record only genuinely unrelated failures. Verify `git diff --check` and confirm the final test count matches the original 28 tests with no changed production files.

## Completion criteria

- Every lifecycle invariant has a clear behavioral home: selection/catalog, switching/order, or cancellation/recovery.
- Ordinary sidebar navigation is separate from Workspace lifecycle coverage.
- Tests remain App renderer/integration tests and retain their user-visible/domain-invariant assertions.
- The split changes no production code, RPC contracts, or workspace transition semantics.

title: Split App workspace transition tests by behavior
